item.title: Bangladesh’s energy bill to swell further
item.description: With the Middle East conflict getting into another uncertain episode, Bangladesh is being forced into paying even more to keep the lights and the factories on, or cut back on the use and watch output slow....

item.title: StanChart strengthens global standing with multiple int’l accolades
item.description: Standard Chartered Bangladesh has secured multiple prestigious international awards, further cementing its position as one of the country’s leading financial institutions and a trusted partner in Bangladesh’s economic journey....

item.description: Jiban Bima Corporation held its ‘Half-Yearly Business Review Meeting 2026’ on Saturday at its head office, said a press release....

item.description: IFIC Bank PLC signed an agreement with United Healthcare Services Limited to ensure enhanced healthcare facilities for the Bbank’s customers and employees, said a press release....

item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC on Saturday concluded its two-day ‘Half-Yearly Business Development Conference’ for its Chattogram region, said a press release....

item.description: M Nasim Ali, newly appointed chairman of Sadharan Bima Corporation, was accorded a warm reception and presented with floral greetings on Sunday by officers and employees of the corporation....City& Local Guides

item.description: The 77th meeting of the executive committee of the Central Shariah Board for Islamic Banks of Bangladesh was held in the boardroom of Pubali Bank PLC’s head office on Thursday....

item.description: Unilever Bangladesh Limited on Saturday won ‘Most Sustainable Company of the Year’ and ‘Most Sustainable FMCG Company of the Year’ titles at SDG Brand Champion Awards 2026, said a press release....

item.title: Remittance inflows thru BKB hit $4.1b in FY26
item.description: Bangladesh Krishi Bank collected $4.1 billion, equivalent to Tk 50,249 crore, in remittances in the recently concluded financial year 2025-26, said a press release....

item.title: Railway earnings rise, but income trails expenditure
item.description: Bangladesh Railway’s revenue increased by Tk 221 crore in the 2025-26 fiscal year, but it continued to spend significantly more than it earned despite a modest improvement in its operating ratio....Government

item.title: Rupali Bank holds 40th AGM
item.description: Rupali Bank PLC on Sunday held its 40th annual general meeting in a hybrid format at its head office at Dilkusha in the capital, said a press release....

item.title: PRE-LDC GRADUATION: Govt drafting 5-year plan to boost trade capacity
item.description: The government is preparing a five-year action plan to strengthen Bangladesh’s trade capacity, improve investment climate and enhance international competitiveness as the country prepares to graduate from the least developed country category, according to the commerce ministry....

item.title: 59 brands win SDG Brand Champion awards
item.description: At the 4th edition of AkijBashir Group presents SDG Brand Champion Awards 2026 on Saturday, 59 sustainable brand initiatives were awarded, said a press release....

item.title: Insurance stocks lead DSE declines amid profit-taking
item.description: Insurance stocks dominated the list of top price losers on the Dhaka Stock Exchange on the first trading day of the week as investors continued to profit following a recent rally in the sector....Foreignaffairs analysis

item.title: CAAB warns against unauthorised airport ground handling services
item.description: The Civil Aviation Authority of Bangladesh has warned airlines and service providers against engaging in unauthorised ground handling and other operational services at the country’s airports, saying that violations would invite legal and enforcement action....News

item.title: Dhaka, ILO reaffirm labour reform pledges
item.description: Bangladesh and the International Labour Organisation reaffirmed their commitment to deepening cooperation on labour sector reforms, workers’ rights, employment generation and compliance with international labour standards, as the country seeks to accelerate ongoing reforms in the face of evolving global labour market challenges....

item.title: BB cracks down on 29 banks over bad loans
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has stepped up pressure on the country’s weakest lenders, asking 29 banks with default loan ratios above 10 per cent to prepare practical, time-bound plans to reduce their non-performing loans and warned of strict regulatory action if they fail to meet the targets....

item.title: Rain that nourishes the earth
item.description: FOR thousands of years, men wrote history. The word history does not literally mean his story. But as a metaphor, it feels painfully appropriate. Because for most of recorded civilisation, the people who held the pen, interpreted the scriptures, wrote the laws, occupied the universities and decided......Educationnews

item.title: Roots of Srebrenica’s genocide
item.description: THE Srebrenica genocide was the result of long-standing tensions and a deliberate political agenda. Under Tito’s Yugoslavia (1945–1980), Orthodox Serbs, Catholic Croats and Muslim Bosniaks co-existed. After Yugoslavia’s collapse, hardline nationalists, led by the Serb Democratic Party, or the......

item.title: Living with monsoon floods
item.description: EVERY year, as monsoon clouds gather over the Bay of Bengal and rivers begin to swell, Bangladesh enters a season of both promise and apprehension. The rains replenish rivers, sustain agriculture and support millions of rural livelihoods. Yet they also bring widespread disruption, displacement and......

item.title: Foundational learning crisis
item.description: BANGLADESH has long celebrated its achievements in expanding access to primary education. Enrolment rates have risen dramatically over the past three decades, gender parity has largely been achieved, and almost every child now has a primary school within reach. These are important......

item.title: No ambiguity over war hero memorial
item.description: THE dismantling of the sculpture and memorial square erected in honour of Bir Sreshtha Hamidur Rahman, one of the seven recipients of the highest gallantry award for his role in the liberation war, has elicited conflicting statements from various authorities, one passing......

item.title: Dengue outbreak in coastal region warrants action
item.description: THE rapid spread of dengue across the coastal belt has exposed a worrying paradox. Rainwater harvesting, which sustains millions of people facing worsening salinity intrusion and freshwater scarcity, now provides for ideal breeding grounds for Aedes mosquitoes because of unsuitable water......

item.title: Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup
item.description:
Ferran Torres scored an extra-time winner as Spain defeated holders Argentina 1-0 to win the World Cup on Sunday and shatter Lionel Messi's dreams of a triumphant farewell to football's greatest stage.
...News

item.title: Israel PM’s office slams NY mayor over Netanyahu arrest remarks
item.description: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday denounced New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani after he said he considering whether to arrest the Israeli leader during an upcoming UN summit....

item.title: Fire forces evacuations in south France town
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire in southern France forced the evacuation of dozens of homes on Sunday as more than 600 firefighters and a fleet of water-bombing aircraft battled to contain the blaze, authorities said....

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ storms N American Box Office
item.description: Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ sailed straight to the top of the North American box office, bringing home $124.5 million in its opening weekend – the best live action debut of the year, industry estimates showed Sunday....

item.description: Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology hosted its annual technology festival titled CSE Fest 2026 on its campus in Dhaka on Saturday....Soccer

item.description: The primary and mass education ministry has recently changed the formation of committees for the posting of newly recruited assistant teachers and transfer of all serving teachers atgovernmentprimary schools across the country amid criticisms....

item.title: US says service member killed in controlled detonation in north Iraq
item.description: A US service member died during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian drone in northern Iraq, the military said Sunday....

item.title: AIUB participates in QS Africa Forum 2026
item.description: American International University-Bangladesh has recently participated at the QS Africa Forum 2026 at the Sheraton Addis in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia....

item.description: Agriculture, fisheries and livestock minister Mohammed Aminur Rashid has urged higher education institutions to play a more effective role in creating skilled human resources for production-oriented sectors, including agriculture, fisheries and livestock....Maps

item.description: The BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology concluded its two-day BUFT National Apparel Festival 2026, drawing over 2,000 undergraduate students from 32 universities and colleges across the country, on its campus in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: 5 killed in Russian strikes on Turkish cargo ship: Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine’s navy said Sunday that Russian missile strikes on a Turkish-owned cargo ship carrying grain had killed five people on board, while another five crew members were missing....

item.description: The certificate distribution ceremony for the masters of professional banking programme was held at the Professor Abdullah Farouk Multipurpose Hall of Dhaka University on Saturday....

item.title: Minister launches ‘one child, one tree’ initiative in Manikganj
item.description: Civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam inaugurated the one child, one tree programme at Nabagram Government Primary School under Manikganj Sadar in the district on Saturday....Politics

item.title: Committee formed to study int’l bond market borrowing
item.description: The government has formed a committee to explore borrowing from the international bond market to meet its foreign currency needs as interest rates on loans from multilateral agencies continue to rise, Finance Division officials said on Sunday....

item.title: PFEC Global to host edn expo in city on Aug 3
item.description: International education consultancy PFEC Global will organise the Global Education Expo 2026 in Dhaka on August 3 to help Bangladeshi students explore higher education opportunities abroad....

item.title: HC stays proceedings in fraud case against Somoy TV directors
item.description: The High Court on Sunday stayed the proceedings of a fraud and criminal breach of trust case filed by Ahmed Jobaer, former director of Somoy Television Limited, against the company’s chairman and four directors....

item.title: Nat’l unity sought for July charter implementation
item.description: Leaders from ruling and opposition political parties, at separate discussions in the capital Dhaka on Sunday, called for greater unity among political parties to implement the July National Charter and establish a discrimination-free democratic Bangladesh....History

item.title: Nearly 9pc measles deaths were among vaccinated patients: study
item.description: About 8.64 per cent of measles patients who died during the country’s recent outbreak had received at least one dose of the measles vaccine, according to a study....News

item.title: Putin meets N Korea FM, hails war support
item.description: Russian president Vladimir Putin on Sunday thanked North Korea for supporting Moscow’s war on Ukraine as he met visiting foreign minister Choe Son Hui....

item.title: 4 killed in road accidents in 3 districts
item.description: At least four people were killed and 64 others injured in separate road accidents in Jamalpur, Chattogram and Manikganj districts on Sunday....

item.title: Iraq PM to visit Iran this week
item.description: Iraqi prime minister Ali al-Zaidi is scheduled to visit Iran later this week following a trip to the US where he met President Donald Trump, Iraqi state media reported Sunday....

item.title: DHAKA EV BUS PROJECT: Ministry seeks subsidy, experts call it unjustified
item.description: The road transport ministry has requested subsidies for the proposed 400 electric buses on four Dhaka routes, but experts oppose the request, arguing that EV service should cost half as much as diesel-run bus service....

item.title: Ctg seeks urgent flood relief amid supply shortages
item.description: The Chattogram divisional commissioner’s office on Sunday sought urgent relief assistance from thegovernmentamid reports of inadequate supplies for flood victims, as the region reeled from relentless rainfall, landslides and flash floods....Arabs& Middle Easterners

item.title: Two missing as trawler capsizes after collision with launch at Sadarghat
item.description: Two people went missing after a trawler bound for Cumilla sank in the Buriganga River following a collision with a launch at Shyambazar Ghat in the capital's Sadarghat area on Sunday morning......
